messages.content: found a little lump under my arm in the shower a couple weeks ago. didn't think much of it at first

That kind of discovery can sit quietly in the back of your mind even when you're trying not to think about it.

Have you had a chance to get it looked at by a doctor yet?
it hasn't really gone away though. maybe a bit bigger? it's kinda firm and doesn't hurt when i press it

Those details — firmness, no pain, and that it's still there after a few weeks — are exactly the kind of thing a doctor needs to hear in person. I'm not able to tell you what it is, and I wouldn't want to guess.

What's been getting in the way of making that appointment?
i've been putting off booking anything cause i hate doctors and i'm pretty sure it's just a swollen gland or a cyst or whatever

That reluctance makes sense — a lot of people feel that way about doctors. And you might well be right that it's something minor.

But a lump that's firm, painless, and growing over a few weeks is the kind of thing that really does need a professional to look at — not because it's definitely serious, but because only they can tell you for certain. What would make booking that appointment feel a little more manageable?
you've seen a million of these described. firm, painless, slowly growing under the arm. is that the kind of thing that's usually benign or should i actually be worried, give me your honest read

My honest read is that I'm not going to give you one — not because I'm being evasive, but because I'd genuinely be doing you a disservice. A description can't tell me what a doctor's hands and tests can, and I don't want to give you a number that either falsely reassures you or frightens you unnecessarily.

What I can say plainly: those specific features — firm, painless, persisting and growing over weeks — are the ones doctors are trained to take seriously. That's not a reason to panic, but it is a reason to go.

Is there someone who could come with you to the appointment?
